issues: The pages are now convincingly in the reference family, but they still fall short of near-exact matching in the final calibration details: the palette relationships, ornament spacing, and local composition tension are not yet fully at the level of the source images.
The refactor page still has a little too much order in the middle-to-lower transition. The reference feels slightly more irregular and hand-composed.
The bestiary page is very close, but the framing and ornament around the dominant specimen can still be tuned to feel a touch less schematic and more fully fused with the manuscript surface.
strengths: The Seraph kit is materially stronger and genuinely reusable across more than these two pages.
Storybook now exposes meaningful motif-level Seraph kit pieces rather than only page-level assemblies.
The page hierarchy, hero framing, and artifact dominance are all much closer to the references than earlier passes.
A11y and dark-surface state quality are improved without losing the manuscript identity.
a11yRisks: A few small metadata contexts may still benefit from one final contrast nudge, but the remaining issues are narrower now.
No deterministic visual validation exists in this agent-only loop, so final confidence is still judgment-based rather than measured.
nextEdits: Do one final page-calibration pass using the stronger kit: tighten palette hierarchy, local ornament spacing, and the irregular manuscript rhythm in the refactor page.
Tune the bestiary hero framing and surrounding annotations one last time so the dominant specimen feels less boxed and more fused with the folio.
Make only high-impact visual calibration edits now; the kit itself is strong enough.
